channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: La muerte de Joan Sebastian Guerrero en Maine desata nuevas preguntas sobre ICE
item.description: La muerte de Joan Sebastián Durán Guerrero, padre de familia y trabajador con permiso legal, ha profundizado el debate sobre el alcance y la violencia de la ofensiva migratoria.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales, Jenna Russell and Jacey Fortin

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Un altar conmemorativo para Joan Sebastián Durán Guerrero en la intersección donde recibió los disparos de ICE en Biddeford, Maine.

item.title: Estos animales eran diestros mucho antes de que hubiera manos
item.description: Un grupo de científicos propone que unos fósiles descubiertos hace poco podrían ser la evidencia más antigua de “lateralidad” de comportamiento en los animales.
item.dc:creator: Jack Tamisiea

item.title: Paramedics Help Save Lives in New York. Can They Afford to Live There?
item.description: Some of New York City’s emergency medical workers make just $18 an hour. Their boss, Zohran Mamdani, has staked his mayoralty on making New York City more affordable for all.
item.dc:creator: Eliza Shapiro and Vincent Alban

media:content.media:description: Taysha Soto has spent the last two years in close touch with life and death in New York City as an emergency medical technician.

item.title: Mamdani Will Advance Bronx Bus Lane Project to Improve Transit Speeds
item.description: The road redesign to prioritize buses, the first such project in the borough, had been put on hold after the Trump administration objected to a similar plan in Manhattan.
item.dc:creator: Liam Stack and Stefanos Chen

media:content.media:credit: Hiroko Masuike/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: In addition to a dedicated “busway” on Tremont Avenue in the Bronx, the Mamdani administration will gather public input on plans to improve bus service on major roads in Brooklyn.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 15, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: Cómo superé mi adicción al algoritmo sin ayuda de la tecnología
item.description: Probé muchos trucos para dejar de usar tanto las redes sociales. Lo que mejor me funcionó fue un simple ajuste en mi casa.
item.dc:creator: Meher Ahmad

item.title: La guerra para salvar a las abejas de la invasión de avispones
item.description: A medida que los avispones de patas amarillas se expanden por la región de Lowcountry, en Carolina del Sur, y amenazan la producción local de miel, un equipo estatal de defensores de las abejas corre al rescate.
item.dc:creator: Jack Tamisiea and Sean Rayford

media:content.media:credit: Sean Rayford for The New York Times

item.title: Argentina vs. Inglaterra: un partido de semifinal cargado de historia
item.description: La semifinal del Mundial reaviva décadas de rivalidad marcadas por el enfrentamiento de 1986, la guerra de las Malvinas y la figura de Diego Maradona.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja and Lucía Cholakian Herrera

item.title: Late Night Calls Lindsey Graham’s Senate Seat ‘an Inheritance Jackpot’
item.description: “A Senate seat? Most people just get a garage full of damp boxes,” Michael Kosta said of Graham’s sister filling the late senator’s seat on “The Daily Show.”
item.dc:creator: Trish Bendix
item.category: The Daily Show with Jon Stewart (TV Program)
Late Night with Seth Meyers (TV Program)

item.title: Para desplegar una bandera del Mundial se necesita coordinación (y muchos brazos)
item.description: Las banderas se han convertido en íconos instantáneos de este torneo. Desplegarlas y doblarlas rápidamente en la cancha requiere una cuidadosa coreografía.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei and Scott McIntyre

item.title: This Is a Forever War in the Making
item.description: The vague language of the U.S.-Iran cease-fire memo laid the foundation for the war’s resumption.
item.dc:creator: Ali Vaez
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Kim Hoeckele for The New York Times

item.title: ¿Crees que tu gato se porta bien? Piénsalo dos veces
item.description: Un nuevo estudio revela que, a veces, los gatos se acicalan entre sí para molestarse.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Mitchell Brown

item.title: Rescue Effort Underway After Boat Capsizes Near Alcatraz
item.description: A three-deck pleasure boat carrying 20 people capsized near Alcatraz Island on Tuesday. At least one person died and three were missing, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik

media:content.media:credit: Ian C. Bates for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The boat stalled about 600 yards from Alcatraz Island.

item.title: China’s Economy Grows at Slowest Pace in Years
item.description: Economic growth of 4.3 percent in the second quarter, versus the same period last year, reflected a broad slump outside of the country’s export-oriented manufacturing might.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao

media:content.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Shoppers at an electric car showroom in Shanghai. Chinese consumers have embraced a culture of thriftiness, as many are not feeling the benefits of economic growth.

item.title: Trump recibió 2 millones de dólares de una empresa de Corea del Sur
item.description: El pago se reveló inicialmente en el formulario de divulgación financiera anual del presidente de EE. UU., publicado a finales de junio.
item.dc:creator: Eric Lipton and John Yoon

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El presidente Trump mantiene vínculos financieros personales con casi 30 iniciativas comerciales diferentes con contrapartes en todo el mundo.

item.title: For Argentina, a World Cup Semifinal Against England Isn’t Just Soccer
item.description: Wednesday’s contest for a spot in the final carries the weight of a 1986 contentious clash, a war over the Falklands and a national mythology built around Diego Maradona.
item.dc:creator: Tariq Panja and Lucía Cholakian Herrera

item.title: Quote of the Day: T. Rex is King Once Again on Booming Fossil Market
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Wednesday, July 15, 2026.

item.title: Missing From Prince Harry’s UK Trip: A Reunion With Prince William
item.description: Harry is clearly repairing his relationship with King Charles III, but the rift with his brother remains unresolved.
item.dc:creator: Megan Specia
item.category: Camilla, Queen Consort of the United Kingdom
Charles III, King of the United Kingdom

media:content.media:credit: Alberto Pezzali/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Prince Harry played wheelchair rugby on Friday in Birmingham, central England, to promote the one-year countdown to the 2027 Invictus Games, a sports event for wounded veterans.

item.title: As Argentina Tries to Win the World Cup, the Rest of Latin America Cheers Against It
item.description: Rather than rally behind the tournament’s last Latin American team, other countries in the region are confronting Argentina’s fraught claims to its own identity.
item.dc:creator: James Wagner

media:content.media:credit: Jeff Romance/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Colombia fans in yellow and Argentina fans in stripes watching Argentina playing Cape Verde earlier this month in Miami.

item.title: Alzheimer’s Blood Tests Offer New Promise to Diagnose and Predict the Disease
item.description: New studies are shedding light on how the tests could eventually transform treatment.
item.dc:creator: Dana G. Smith and Pam Belluck

media:content.media:credit: Claire Merchlinsky/The New York Times; Photographs via Getty

item.title: Tech Investors and Longevity Influencers Back Neko Health’s Mega Round
item.description: The health tech start-up co-founded by Spotify’s Daniel Ek is worth billions more after a new $700 million funding round.
item.dc:creator: Bernhard Warner

media:content.media:credit: Sam Bush for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Neko Health, founded by Hjalmar Nilsonne, left, and Daniel Ek, offers full-body diagnostic scans priced to attract more than just affluent patients.

item.title: World Cup 2026: See How Spain Pulled Apart France’s Defense
item.description: View in 3-D how Spain cut through France’s defense to double its lead, securing a victory and a place in the World Cup final.
item.dc:creator: Bora Erden, Malika Khurana, Allison McCann, Karthik Patanjali and Raj Saha

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Former Child Care Worker in Sydney Facing Over 300 Abuse Charges Is Identified
item.description: A Sydney court lifted the suppression order on identifying a man accused of abusing more than 100 children over 16 years.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 15, 2026
item.description: Jonathan Raksin and Jeff Chen enter this week’s constructor rotation.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:credit: Michael Nagle for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Stuck on today’s puzzle? The solve must go on. Read the Wordplay column for hints and answers.

item.title: Polls: What’s Driving Support for Senate Candidates in Battleground States?
item.description: A New York Times/Siena poll of battleground states showed a mix of voter motivations, with the strongest candidates proving more popular than their parties.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Soler, Ruth Igielnik and Christine Zhang

media:content.media:credit: Kerry Trasker/Reuters; Michael A. McCoy, Travis Dove and Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times

item.title: Federal Official Sets Deadlines to Overhaul Rikers Island
item.description: The official, Nicholas Deml, submitted the first in a series of reports to a federal judge, focusing on key areas of improvement, including safety, at the troubled facility.
item.dc:creator: Hurubie Meko

media:content.media:credit: Alex Kent
media:content.media:description: The federal official in charge of overhauling the city jail system laid out his plan to improve five key issues at the Rikers Island jail complex.

item.title: Videos Detail Moments Surrounding Fatal ICE Shooting in Biddeford, Maine
item.description: Footage obtained by The New York Times shows ICE agents in the moments before and after the killing of Joan Sebastian Guerrero. The circumstances of the shooting remain unclear.
item.dc:creator: Allison McCann, Devon Lum, Tim Ouillette, Aric Toler and Leanne Abraham

item.title: Data Centers to Add Billions in Power Costs in 13 States
item.description: A power auction conducted by a giant grid operator is expected to add $6.3 billion in additional charges to consumers and businesses because of electricity needs of data centers.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Penn

media:content.media:credit: Leah Millis/Reuters
media:content.media:description: Northern Virginia is home to several data centers, including the Digital Realty Data Center in Ashburn.

item.title: One Dead and Three Missing in San Francisco Bay After Boat Capsizes Near Alcatraz
item.description: Sixteen other passengers were rescued after the pleasure boat sank in the San Francisco Bay on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Soumya Karlamangla

media:content.media:credit: Ian C. Bates for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The boat stalled about 600 yards from Alcatraz Island.

item.title: U.S. War Against Iran Enters a New Phase
item.description: As President Trump resumes his war, the focus is now on the Strait of Hormuz. But it remains unclear how far the U.S. military will go to exert control.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Eric Schmitt and David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump in the Oval Office on Tuesday. The latest phase of the U.S. military campaign in Iran has a new focus, but not necessarily a clearer strategy.

item.title: As Confirmation Hearing Nears, Todd Blanche’s Role in Epstein Case Draws Scrutiny
item.description: Todd Blanche’s role in interviewing Ghislaine Maxwell, a longtime associate of the convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein, will be front and center as he seeks to become attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett

media:content.media:credit: Alex Kent/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, last month. As the Justice Department’s No. 2 official, he oversaw the release of the Epstein files.

item.title: Trump paga 5,6 millones de dólares a E. Jean Carroll por caso civil
item.description: Un jurado federal determinó que el presidente Trump era responsable de abuso sexual y difamación contra Carroll. Luego de que él agotó todas las vías de apelación el mes pasado, un juez ordenó que se efectuara el pago.
item.dc:creator: Ed Shanahan and Jonah E. Bromwich

media:content.media:credit: Adam Gray/Reuters
media:content.media:description: E. Jean Carroll recibió los 5 millones de dólares que se le ordenó al presidente Trump pagar en una resolución que lo halló responsable de abusar sexualmente de ella y difamarla.

item.title: At War With Iran Again, Trump Finds an Opponent He Cannot Easily Dominate
item.description: The president is accustomed to forcing other countries to bend to his will. But he is struggling to come up with a strategy to extract Iranian concessions as the cease-fire he brokered collapses.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A banner in Tehran last week threatening President Trump.

item.title: House Votes for Permanent Daylight Saving Time
item.description: House Republicans and Democrats united in favor of a bill to eliminate semiannual clock-changing, but it faces an uncertain fate in the Senate.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Diaz

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Capitol in March 2023, when then-Senator Marco Rubio reintroduced the Sunshine Protection Act, to permanently enact Daylight Saving Time and eliminate the twice-a-year clock changes.

item.title: Kevin Warsh Pledges to Bring Inflation Down but Doesn’t Specify How
item.description: Kevin M. Warsh, the new chairman of the Federal Reserve, reiterated his commitment to get inflation down but did not provide specifics during his first testimony to Congress.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

media:content.media:credit: Alex Kent/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: US Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh testifying during a House Financial Services Committee hearing on Capitol Hill today.

item.title: Iran’s Cyberattackers Tracked Phones of U.S. Military Personnel in the Mideast, Data Suggests
item.description: Researchers say Iran’s cyberwarfare capabilities have grown more advanced and aggressive during the war with the United States and Israel.
item.dc:creator: Zane Irwin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Iran used cellphone signals to track U.S. military members at the start of the war, according to newly released data.

item.title: Two Supreme Court Justices Spoke to Raise Security Funds in Rare Congressional Testimony
item.description: Supreme Court Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett spoke in rare congressional testimony to increase security funds amid rising threats.
item.dc:creator: David Cheung

media:content.media:credit: Anna Moneymaker/Getty Images

item.title: An ICE Shooting in Maine Puts Pressure on Senator Susan Collins
item.description: The Democrats hoping to challenge Ms. Collins, a vulnerable Republican, have seized on the fatal incident, drawing attention to her record on immigration matters.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “It’s disappointing to see potential candidates use a tragedy to try to advance a political agenda,” Senator Susan Collins told reporters at the U.S. Capitol on Tuesday.

item.title: Trump cambia su plan en el estrecho de Ormuz y genera inquietud
item.description: Después de abandonar su propuesta de imponer tarifas a los barcos, el presidente plantea acuerdos de inversión con los países del Golfo a cambio de protección.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Gross
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Un día después de decir que cobraría tarifas del 20 por ciento a los barcos a cambio de protección militar estadounidense en el estrecho de Ormuz, Trump dijo que, en su lugar, quería que los Estados del Golfo invirtieran en Estados Unidos.

item.title: Security Cameras Capture Moments After Fatal ICE Shooting in Maine
item.description: Footage from nearby surveillance cameras shows federal agents pulling Joan Sebastian Guerrero from a white car after a shooting in Biddeford, Maine, on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Leventhal

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A memorial for a Mexican immigrant, Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, at the spot where he was shot by an ICE agent in Houston.

item.title: ICE Is Ordered to Pause Most Vehicle Stops
item.description: Also, Trump drops his plan for a Strait of Hormuz fee. Here’s the latest at the end of Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Meridith Kohut for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A memorial for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo in Houston last week.

item.title: Colombian Immigrant Killed by ICE in Maine Had Legal Status, Father Says
item.description: The father of Joan Sebastian Guerrero, the man fatally shot by a federal immigration agent on Monday, said his son had been working two jobs to support his wife and daughter.
item.dc:creator: Christina Morales, Jenna Russell and Jacey Fortin

media:content.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A memorial for Joan Sebastian Guerrero at the intersection where he was shot by ICE in Biddeford, Maine.

item.title: ICE Shootings Put Spotlight on Lack of Body Cameras
item.description: Kristi Noem, the former homeland security secretary, said five months ago that the Trump administration would “rapidly” deploy body cameras to immigration agents across the country. About half still don’t have them.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo

media:content.media:credit: Joseph Prezioso/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Law enforcement officers at the scene of the ICE shooting in Biddeford, Maine, on Tuesday.

item.title: Opportunities Narrow for Women as Hegseth Blocks More Promotions
item.description: For the first time in more than a decade, no active-duty female Navy officers are likely to be promoted to admiral this year.
item.dc:creator: Greg Jaffe
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Alex Kent/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has repeatedly claimed that the military has focused too much on promoting minority and female officers, at the expense of white men.

item.title: Lindsey Graham’s Sister, Darline Graham, Is Sworn In to Finish His Senate Term
item.description: Three days after Lindsey Graham died, his younger sister Darline Graham took the oath to hold his seat until his term ends in January.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Diaz

media:content.media:credit: Alex Kent/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Darline Graham being sworn in by Senator Charles E. Grassley on Tuesday as her husband, Larry Nordone, holds a Bible.

item.description: Axuan Bu, a food stall owner in Jinhua, has absorbed rising egg costs rather than raise prices for customers.

item.title: Climate: An extraordinary White House meeting
item.description: A new book sheds light on President Trump’s affinity for fossil fuels.
item.dc:creator: David Gelles

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Loureiro Fernandez for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An oil refinery in Punto Fijo, Venezuela. In a meeting with the president, the Chevron chief executive pushed for an extension of the company’s license to operate in the country.

item.description: A vendor prepares jianbing, a popular Chinese savory pancake, as rising egg and fuel costs increase operating expenses.
item.dc:creator: Qilai Shen

media:content.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times

item.title: Expelled from China
item.description: A New York Times correspondent was kicked out of the country after reporting on the struggles of everyday Chinese. Here’s her story.
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Kenny Holston/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Tiananmen Square in Beijing in May.

item.title: Lindsey Graham’s Choice
item.description: Would the world have been safer if Graham hadn’t cozied up to Trump?
item.dc:creator: Bret Stephens

media:content.media:credit: Photo illustration by The New York Times. Source photograph: Ukrainian Presidential Press Service/via Reuters
